4fc9535d9cdc.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
4fc9535d9cdc.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
4fc9535d9cdc.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

927da906c1d8.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
927da906c1d8.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

469587c86785.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
469587c86785.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
469587c86785.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

aa79730ab8ae.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
aa79730ab8ae.description: Your 4.7/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P42.9). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
aa79730ab8ae.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

eeb9552e6bb3.title: Ranked #5 of 8 locally — peers are ahead
eeb9552e6bb3.description: At percentile 42.9, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

065974e1604a.title: Accelerate review volume — you're credible but not dominant
065974e1604a.description: At 102 reviews, your rating is stable but not commanding. Venues with 200+ reviews rank higher in Maps. A post-visit review prompt would compound monthly.
